text: Hyperliquid's approach of integrating capital, execution, and user demand into a cohesive system is highlighted as a key factor in its revenue generation efficiency, outperforming other DeFi protocols. The platform's model of directing nearly all revenue towards token buybacks creates a strong feedback loop reinforcing token value and ecosystem growth.

text: Hyperliquid is recognized as the dominant perpetual DEX in 2026, maintaining over 25% market share and outperforming competitors like Aster_DEX and edgeX_exchange. Its success is attributed to real product quality, sustainable tokenomics, and strong user trust, unlike many competitors that suffered post-token launch crashes due to airdrop farming and weak economics. The platform supports a wide range of assets including crypto majors and real-world indices, with continuous innovation such as HIP-3 and HIP-4 upgrades.

text: Hyperliquid's native token HYPE has seen significant accumulation by whales and public figures, with exchange supply dropping by 22.86%. Large buys ranging from $51K to $99K were observed, indicating strong holder conviction. The token price has outperformed major cryptocurrencies since December 2024, supported by daily buybacks funded by protocol fees. The tokenomics emphasize revenue capture and deflationary mechanisms, aligning incentives with holders.
author: elfa_2047164296308912639

text: Hyperliquid has completed a successful year-one retrospective highlighting its unique approach of building revenue first with perpetuals, then EVM infrastructure, and expanding product surfaces. It has achieved $981M chain-only revenue on $5.19B TVL, significantly outperforming Ethereum and Solana in revenue efficiency. The HIP-3 upgrade enabled independent deployers to list diverse perp markets, increasing fee take rates and buybacks of HYPE tokens, which have appreciated strongly. HyperEVM supports DeFi protocols with $1.44B TVL, while the orderbook maintains $3.75B in working capital. The ecosystem is expanding with tokenized equities, real-world assets, and new yield products like haUSDC, which aims to scale capital efficiency and yield generation.
author: elfa_2047462063824568461

text: MantleCore has submitted the draft MIP-34 proposal to authorize Mantle Treasury to lend up to 30k ETH to Aave DAO to address bad debt tied to the April 18, 2026 rsETH bridge exploit on Aave V3. The draft sets a term of up to 36 months, with interest at LIDO+1% APR or other terms to be finalized by the execution team, and allows early repayment without penalty. It also proposes additional collateral from Aave, including 5% of protocol revenue and Aave tokens with a fair market value of at least $11 million, while Mantle would receive delegated voting power of 130k Aave tokens. The proposal is currently under discussion and has not yet entered a formal vote. — link

text: Highlight Clip Father of Smart Contracts: Bitcoin Solves the "Fundamental Flaws" in Monetary History At Bitcoin 2021 on June 6, 2021, Nick Szabo, the "Father of Smart Contracts," stated that Bitcoin addresses the fundamental flaws of precious metals and fiat currency. He explained that its global node network solves insecure, costly transport and storage, while its low-cost, trustless verification reduces systemic fragility. Bitcoin also replaces digital centralization with censorship-resistant decentralization and answers fiat experiments with a fixed, auditable money supply. — link

text: U.S. Soldier Charged With Using Classified Information to Bet on Polymarket, Profiting About $409,000 U.S. prosecutors charged active-duty soldier Gannon Ken Van Dyke with using classified information obtained during a military operation targeting Nicolás Maduro to place bets on related outcomes on the prediction market Polymarket, generating about $409,000 in profit from roughly $33,000 in wagers, and facing charges including violations of the Commodity Exchange Act, wire fraud, and unlawful monetary transactions. The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has also filed a complaint seeking restitution, disgorgement, civil monetary penalties, trading and registration bans, and a permanent injunction. According to Bloomberg, Donald Trump said he would review federal employees' participation in prediction market betting, warning that such platforms could be used to profit from confidential information; a series of well-timed bets has also raised concerns about potential insider trading and regulatory risks. — link

text: Lido Submits Proposal to Join rsETH Relief Effort, Up to 2,500 stETH Contribution Lido has submitted a proposal on the Research Forum to participate in Aave's coordinated rsETH relief effort following this week's Kelp rsETH LayerZero bridge exploit. The proposal seeks authorization for a one-time allocation of up to 2,500 stETH to a dedicated relief vehicle. The contribution would be made only as part of a fully funded recovery package to fully cover the rsETH deficit. The initiative aims to reduce spillover risks across the DeFi ecosystem and support an orderly resolution for affected users. The EtherFi Foundation plans to contribute 5,000 ETH to a dedicated relief vehicle to help close the rsETH shortfall and reduce bad debt risk across the DeFi ecosystem. EtherFi is working with Aave and other participants to address the shortfall following this week's exploit. Ethena is participating in Aave's coordinated rsETH relief effort, with its contribution going towards restoring rsETH backing. Aave founder Stani Kulechov said he is personally contributing 5,000 ETH to the DeFi United relief effort and is working with partners to formalize additional commitments, aiming to resolve the situation and restore normal market conditions. LayerZero said it will contribute to an industry-wide recovery effort to help restore rsETH backing, in coordination with Aave and other ecosystem participants. — link

text: Knight Frank's 20th edition Wealth Report 2026 Wealth is exploding, but concentrating in the US while new hubs emerge • Global UHNWIs (Networth of >US$30M+) hit 713,626 in 2026 and will reach 948,241 by 2031. The US now creates 41% of all new UHNWIs and will hold 41% of the global total by 2031 • The fastest-growing wealth markets are Indonesia (+82%), Saudi Arabia (+63%), Poland (+63%), Vietnam (+59%), and Australia (+59%) The ultra-wealthy are now ultra-mobile, reshaping prime property globally • Tax pressure and frictionless tech are driving a "dip-in, dip-out" lifestyle where UHNWIs maintain multiple global bases rather than single residences • Global prime prices rose 3.2% in 2025, led by Dubai (+25.1%) and Tokyo (+58.5%), while London (-4.7%) bleeds capital to Milan, Monaco, Dubai, and Miami - Branded residences, turnkey homes, and private members' clubs are booming as a result Luxury is shifting from "stuff" to transformation + private capital now runs commercial real estate • Experience-led spending (wellness, longevity, exclusive clubs, belonging) overtakes over consumption • Simultaneously, private capital has become the biggest buyer of commercial real estate for four straight years, with family offices (now ~10,000 globally) professionalising into serious investment platforms targeting data centres, value-add real estate, and co-investments

text: Polymarket's Paris Weather Market Manipulated via Physical Hair Dryer Tampering to Net $34,000 According to Le Monde, An individual exploited an unguarded Météo France temperature sensor near the runway at Charles de Gaulle (CDG) Airport by using a portable heating device—suspected to be a hair dryer—to temporarily spike the reading. The individual purchased long-shot outcomes (such as "22°C") at extremely low prices on April 6 and April 15, then manipulated the sensor to trigger a market settlement in his favor, netting a total of approximately $34,000. Météo France has filed a criminal complaint with the French authorities. — link

text: Pornhub Replaces USDT with USDC for Creator Payouts According to Protos, the world's largest adult website, Pornhub, has replaced USDT with USDC for creator payouts. A circulated email says the move is aimed at improving payment reliability, while describing USDC as a regulated stablecoin compliant with MiCA. USDT is no longer listed as a payout option. — link

text: The KelpDAO hacker has swapped nearly all of its 75,700 ETH holdings, worth about $175 million, into BTC over roughly a day and a half. The cross-chain swaps were primarily routed through THORChain. Due to the sheer size of the transactions, the laundering activity also generated about $800 million in volume and roughly $910,000 in platform fee revenue for THORChain. — link

text: How someone manipulated weather in Paris to win a bet An individual exploited Polymarket's Paris daily high temperature markets by briefly heating the sensor with a hair dryer to trigger long-shot payouts totaling ~$34k The sensor's unguarded location allowed a quick temperature spike that registered as the daily max before normalizing, as shown in Polymarket charts with sharp late probability shifts for higher ranges like 22°C

text: Circle's Chief Economist puts up a proposal on AAVE USDC market Aave's USDC pool has been maxed out for 4 days (borrowers have drained it and new lenders aren't depositing, so people trying to withdraw are stuck in a queue) Suggests making the interest rate spike much higher when the pool is this full (max rate from ~14% to ~48% for lenders) to attract fresh deposits, which would refill the pool and normalise rates

text: The Art of Exit Liquidity VCs inflated private companies to trillions and now seek exit liquidity by selling to public markets and retail Force passive retail capital (401ks, index funds) to buy insider positions at peak valuations. This breaks the implicit American bargain where public markets served as the de facto welfare state, accelerating the K-shaped divide and fueling visible backlash against the tech/VC class

text: Highlight Clip Paul Chan: HK's First Two Stablecoin Licenses are Just the Beginning Paul Chan Mo-po stated that Hong Kong remains fully open to Web3 firms, emphasizing that the true value of blockchain and digital assets lies in solving real-world problems. Following the recent issuance of two stablecoin licenses, he highlighted their significant potential to streamline cross-border payments. Having already issued over $2 billion in tokenized bonds, the city is now forming a dedicated committee to explore the synergy between Web3 and AI. — link
